zh.Frenze, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可福倫
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Margaret Heigham於1425年出生在英國素福爾可加澤利時，她的父親Thomas Higham III27歲，母親Alice Boyes28歲。她於大約1447年與Sir John Barton Blennerhasset在英國結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1473年10月6日在她的家鄉享年48歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可加澤利。

zh-hans.Frenze, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom: 英国英格兰諾福爾可福倫
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Margaret Heigham于1425年出生在英国素福爾可加澤利时，她的父亲Thomas Higham III27岁，母亲Alice Boyes28岁。她于大约1447年与Sir John Barton Blennerhasset在英国结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1473年10月6日在她的家乡享年48岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可加澤利。

nameMeanings.name: Heigham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places called Higham (Lancashire, Yorkshire, Derbyshire, Leicestershire, Northamptonshire, Bedfordshire, Warwickshire, Essex, and Kent) or from Heigham in Norfolk. Most of the placenames derive from Old English hēah ‘high’ + hām ‘village, homestead’, though the Norfolk placename may come from Old English hecg ‘hedge’ or hecc ‘hatch, gate’ + hām.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta

The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Margaret Heigham was born in 1425, in Gazeley, Suffolk, England, her father, Thomas Higham III, was 27 and her mother, Alice Boyes, was 28. She married Sir John Barton Blennerhasset about 1447, in England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 6 October 1473, in her hometown, at the age of 48, and was buried in Gazeley, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Margaret Heigham est née en 1425, à Gazeley, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Higham III, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Alice Boyes, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Barton Blennerhasset environ 1447, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 6 octobre 1473, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterrée à Gazeley, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Margaret Heigham 1425 in Gazeley, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Higham III 27 und ihre Mutter Alice Boyes 28. Sie hat um 1447 in England Sir John Barton Blennerhasset geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 6. Oktober 1473 im Alter von 48 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Gazeley, Suffolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Margaret Heigham è nata il nel 1425 nel Gazeley, Suffolk, England, sua padre, Thomas Higham III, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Alice Boyes, aveva 28 anni. Lei sposato Sir John Barton Blennerhasset circa 1447, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 6 ottobre 1473, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 48 anni, e fu sepolta a Gazeley, Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Margaret Heighamが1425年にイギリススフォルクガズエリーで生まれた時，父Thomas Higham IIIは27歳，母Alice Boyesは28歳であった。約1447年にイギリスでSir John Barton Blennerhassetと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1473年10月6日に故郷で48歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクガズエリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Margaret Heigham(이)가 1425년 영국 수폴크 게이즈리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Higham III(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Boyes(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 대략 1447년 영국에서 Sir John Barton Blennerhasset(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1473년 10월 6일 그녀의 고향에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 게이즈리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Margaret Heigham nasceu em 1425, em Gazeley, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Higham III, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Alice Boyes, tinha 28 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John Barton Blennerhasset aproximadamente 1447, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 6 de outubro de 1473, em sua cidade natal, com 48 anos, e foi sepultada em Gazeley, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Margaret Heigham родилась в 1425 г. в местности газели, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Higham III было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Boyes, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Barton Blennerhasset около 1447 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 6 октября 1473 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 48 лет и была похоронена в местности газели, суффолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Margaret Heigham nació en 1425, en Gazeley, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Higham III, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Alice Boyes, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Sir John Barton Blennerhasset aproximadamente 1447, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 6 de octubre de 1473, en su pueblo, a la edad de 48 años, y fue sepultada en Gazeley, Suffolk, Inglaterra.
